architecture.summary: A variational deep logic network combining a self-attention-based deep neural network for implicit feature learning with a relational logic network for explicit reasoning about entity-relation correlations. The two components are trained interactively via variational EM, allowing information to flow from neural representations into logic constraints and vice versa, enabling joint entity and relation extraction with structured reasoning.
trained_components: self-attention-based deep neural network for feature learning

biblio.title: Variational Deep Logic Network for Joint Inference of Entities and Relations

claims.statement: The model incorporates both representation learning and relational reasoning via the variational EM algorithm to jointly perform entity extraction and relation prediction while explicitly encoding correlations between entity types and relations

A deep neural network learns high-level features with implicit interactions via the self-attention mechanism, while a relational logic network explicitly exploits target interactions between entities and relations

The two components are trained interactively to bring the best of both worlds, addressing limitations of pure neural approaches that fail to encode intensive correlations and rule-based approaches that suffer from error propagation and inflexibility
The method is evaluated on fine-grained sentiment terms extraction, end-to-end relation prediction, and end-to-end event extraction tasks
